Name:
The Massacre
(St. Valentine's Day Massacre)
Notes:
(02/06/2003) (Get Rich Or Die Tryin' officially releases)
(03/03/2005) (The Massacre officially releases)
Type: After 50 released his monstrous debut in 2003, demand was high for 50 content. He would help his fellow G-Unit members get off the ground before releasing The Massacre in 2005. According to 50, the album had a bit of a different direction at first, focusing more on imperfections and with more generally soft content on the album then release, at least for the singles. However, after giving some songs the The Game for "The Documentary", he changed his direction and got songs like "Candy Shop" and "Just A Lil' Bit" among others on the album. The album was supposed to come out in February 2005, however this was delayed to give Game's Documentary more breathing room. 50 was quite unhappy with this and leaked Disco Inferno early, along with Candy Shop months later.

Name: St. Valentine's Day Massacre [V1]

(07/01/2008) (T.O.S. (Terminate On Sight) officially releases)
Type: T.O.S. (Terminate on Sight) is the second and final studio album by rap group G-Unit. It was the group's first album in five years since their previous album, Beg for Mercy. The title of the album was originally announced to be Shoot to Kill and then Lock & Load, with the eventual name being Terminate on Sight. The album was originally scheduled to be released on June 24, 2008, but was rescheduled to July 1.

I just want to create something that’s out of the box, that ain’t the same style of music or same kinds of music [that I normally make]. I’m using different song formats… it’s still hip-hop music, it’s still 50 Cent content, it’s just written in a different way [from the traditional 16 bar format].” Songs that released that could have been for the album are "The Enforcer", "Outlaw" and "Hard Rock". According to 50 in 2012, the album was on his iPod. Also according to 50 Cent he recorded 41 tracks for this album.

Too $hort described the song as having his best verse ever, and confirmed the hook was already present. King T also commented on this song saying it's "the best Too Short song I’ve ever heard in my life". It's possible 50 Cent and Lil Kim are on this version as well. This version is the last version Too $hort did, as he had 3 sessions for his verse. From Summer 2011.
